tools.name: clang-format
tools.scope: C, C++, CUDA, proto, textproto, Java files

linting.notes: All linting is orchestrated via pre-commit hooks. The configuration excludes src/grpc_generated directory. Flake8 uses max-line-length=88 (black compatible) with specific ignores (E203, E501). Mypy only runs on genai-perf subdirectory.

setup_required: Build and install tritonclient package via CMake
testing.notes: Tests are integration tests that require: (1) the tritonclient package to be built and installed via CMake build process, (2) a running Triton Inference Server instance. Python tests: test_inference_server_client.py (HTTP client), test_shared_memory.py (shared memory), test_cuda_shared_memory.py (CUDA shared memory, requires PyTorch). C++ tests: cc_client_test.cc (gtest), client_timeout_test.cc, memory_leak_test.cc. Tests cannot run without full build infrastructure and external Triton server.

ci.notes: Two workflows run on pull_request events. The pre-commit workflow runs all pre-commit hooks (isort, black, flake8, clang-format, codespell, mypy, and various file checks). CodeQL analyzes Python code for security issues. Both are gating checks required for merge. No test execution in CI - tests require Triton server infrastructure.

build.notes: Complex CMake-based build system for multi-language client libraries. Requires C++17, various dependencies (grpc, protobuf, curl, re2). Python package built via setup.py with VERSION env var. Build fetches third-party dependencies from triton-inference-server/third_party repo. Full build requires significant setup and compilation time.

conventions.test_naming: Python: TestCamelCase classes with test_snake_case methods. C++: Google Test with TEST() macros
conventions.import_style: Python: isort with black profile, multi-line with trailing commas. C++: standard includes first, then local
conventions.notes: Python code follows black formatting (88 char line length). C++ follows .clang-format configuration. Excludes src/grpc_generated (auto-generated code). Copyright headers required on all source files.

setup_commands: apt-get update && apt-get install -y git make

lint_commands.notes: All checks passed: isort, black, flake8, clang-format, codespell, mypy (skipped - no applicable files), and pre-commit-hooks

test_commands.notes: Tests require tritonclient package to be built/installed first (CMake build). Import errors: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tritonclient'. Tests also require running Triton Inference Server instance.

container_recipe.notes: Linting works perfectly in container. Testing requires full CMake build of tritonclient package, which involves C++ compilation and external dependencies (grpc, protobuf, etc.). Tests are integration tests requiring Triton Inference Server to be running. For patch validation, linting is sufficient to catch style issues.

results.command: apt-get update && apt-get install -y git make

results.notes: Tests require tritonclient package build (CMake + C++ compilation) and Triton server. Cannot validate without full infrastructure.

validation.summary: Linting validated successfully (all checks pass). Testing requires CMake build and Triton server infrastructure.

gaps: Tests require full CMake build with C++ compilation (cannot run in simple Python container)
Tests are integration tests requiring external Triton Inference Server instance
No unit tests that can run standalone without server infrastructure
C++ tests require compilation and cannot be validated without full build environment
CUDA tests require PyTorch and GPU support
No Makefile targets for simplified testing
Complex build system with many CMake options and external dependency fetching
No coverage configuration or coverage reporting
